Case study / 04Education · Archived
Student resource platform

Ssteh

A community-minded web platform for making useful high-school learning resources easier to find and share.

Working draft — project data to be expanded
Scope
Web platform and content model
Role
Web development
Period
2022
Status
Archived
On this page
  1. The brief 01
  2. Content model 02
  3. Decisions 03
  4. Reflection 04
01

Useful material is only useful when students can find it.

Study notes and recommendations often circulate in private chats, disappear in old folders or arrive without the context needed to judge them.

Ssteh explored a shared home for curated resources and collaborative learning. The product direction centered organization, approachable discovery and contribution by the students who understand the need firsthand.

Discover

Browse by need

Organize resources around the way students look for subjects and materials.

Understand

Useful context

Show enough information to evaluate a resource before opening it.

Contribute

Community input

Make sharing material a clear, repeatable part of the platform.

Curate

Quality over volume

Treat organization and relevance as product features, not afterthoughts.

02

A simple path from question to resource.

The concept reduces navigation depth and keeps the core object—the learning resource—at the center of the experience.

Content journey / simplified
SubjectArea of study
TopicSpecific need
ResourceMaterial & context
FeedbackCommunity signal

Project surface

  • Responsive web
  • Resource taxonomy
  • Search
  • Community content
  • Archive
03

Make the library feel approachable.

Structure

Familiar categories

Use the language of school subjects and topics rather than an abstract content system.

Density

Scan before reading

Cards expose the most useful metadata without making the page feel like a spreadsheet.

Contribution

Lower the barrier

Sharing should ask for only the information needed to keep the library useful.

Tone

Peer, not institution

The interface should feel helpful and direct rather than formal or administrative.

04

A useful lesson in content-led products.

Ssteh showed that the interface is only half the product. A resource platform succeeds through the quality, structure and continued care of what it contains.

Learning

Taxonomy matters

A clear content model makes both discovery and future development easier.

Next evidence

Add the record

Document verified user numbers, contributions and feedback when historical data is available.

Next project / 05
